datasets.title: Metagenomic analysis of fecal microbiome as a tool towards targeted non-invasive biomarkers for colorectal cancer
datasets.description: Shotgun metagenomic profiling of stool from CRC patients vs. controls (China + Denmark validation) to identify diagnostic biomarkers.

datasets.title: Reproducibility of associations between the human gut microbiome and colorectal cancer (Washington DC cohort)
datasets.description: Whole-genome shotgun metagenomics of stool from 52 CRC cases + 52 matched controls (Washington DC), compared to a French CRC cohort.

datasets.title: Whole shotgun metagenomic sequencing for 1,004 individuals belonging to the TwinsUK cohort
datasets.description: Shotgun metagenomes of ~1,004 TwinsUK twins, used to study links between gut microbiome composition and host serum/faecal metabolome.

datasets.title: Mediterranean diet intervention in overweight and obese subjects (Meslier et al. 2020)
datasets.description: Shotgun metagenomics of stool samples from a Mediterranean-diet intervention in overweight/obese adults — looks at how an 8-week dietary shift reshapes the gut microbiome and metabolome independently of energy intake. 244 samples across 82 subjects, sampled at baseline, 4 weeks and 8 weeks.

datasets.title: Liver cirrhosis metagenome-wide association study
datasets.description: Faecal shotgun metagenomics of 98 liver-cirrhosis patients vs. 83 controls; built a 2.69 M-gene reference catalogue and identified cirrhosis-associated taxa and functions.

datasets.title: Intentionally contaminated samples from the metaquantibiote study and their uncontaminated counterparts
datasets.description: Pairs of stool metagenomes deliberately cross-contaminated in the wet lab plus their clean counterparts, used to benchmark contamination-detection tools.

datasets.title: Mother-to-infant microbial transmission from different body sites shapes the developing infant gut microbiome
datasets.description: Shotgun metagenomics of 25 mother–infant pairs across multiple body sites (stool, tongue dorsum, vagina, skin) to track vertical strain transmission.
datasets.citation: Ferretti et al. 2018, Cell Host & Microbe

datasets.title: Early-life metagenomes from infant fecal samples with matched maternal samples (Plate 2)
datasets.description: Plate 2 sub-cohort of the genome-resolved shotgun metagenomics dataset Lou et al. used to demonstrate well-to-well contamination in shotgun sequencing.

datasets.title: Early-life metagenomes from infant fecal samples with matched maternal samples (Plate 3)
datasets.description: Plate 3 sub-cohort of the genome-resolved shotgun metagenomics dataset Lou et al. used to demonstrate well-to-well contamination — same data as the bundled tutorial demo.

datasets.title: Simulated cross-sample contamination benchmark on two CRC cohorts (Meteor profiling)
datasets.description: In-silico contamination benchmark built on top of two human-gut CRC cohorts (young-onset CRC, Yang et al. 2021; Japanese colonoscopy, Yachida et al. 2019), profiled with Meteor.
datasets.citation: Yang et al. 2021, Nat Commun · Yachida et al. 2019, Nat Med

datasets.title: Simulated cross-sample contamination benchmark on two CRC cohorts (MetaPhlAn4, raw profiles)
datasets.description: Same in-silico contamination benchmark, profiled with MetaPhlAn4. Raw profiles, CroCoDeEL run without --filter-low-ab.
datasets.citation: Yang et al. 2021, Nat Commun · Yachida et al. 2019, Nat Med

datasets.title: Simulated cross-sample contamination benchmark on two CRC cohorts (MetaPhlAn4 + filter-low-ab 20)
datasets.description: Same in-silico contamination benchmark, profiled with MetaPhlAn4. Raw profiles, CroCoDeEL run with --filter-low-ab 20 (recommended for MetaPhlAn4).
datasets.citation: Yang et al. 2021, Nat Commun · Yachida et al. 2019, Nat Med

datasets.title: Simulated cross-sample contamination benchmark on two CRC cohorts (Sylph profiling)
datasets.description: Same in-silico contamination benchmark, profiled with Sylph against GTDB r214.
datasets.citation: Yang et al. 2021, Nat Commun · Yachida et al. 2019, Nat Med
